localAudit.needle: pc34 table up arrow moves forward
projectile matching direction keeps command queued

narrowedBlocker: Keyboard-buffer transcripts are still non-promotable until they prove M528 key extraction, F0361 table match plus G0432/G2153 queue write, and the following F0380 pop/decrement/dispatch before the post-command F0128/F0097 state-delta frame.
nonClaims: no original runtime capture was run
no claim that F0365/F0366 source handlers are defective
promotionPredicate: capture/log the concrete M528_GetCharacterInKeyboardBuffer value after M527 reports non-empty
prove that value matches COMMAND.C G0459 PC-34 table row before F0361 exits
prove F0361 writes G0432 and increments G2153_i_QueuedCommandsCount
prove the next F0380 sees non-zero G2153, loads the same command, decrements G2153, then dispatches F0365/F0366
only then bind the following F0128/F0097 frame to the changed direction/X/Y state
rejectAsNonPromotable: route keylog labels without M528/F0361 key-code value
F0361 entry stop with no G0432 write and no G2153 increment
F0380 stop where G2153_i_QueuedCommandsCount is zero or movement-disabled gate bypasses dispatch
F0128/F0097 frame evidence without the preceding F0380 pop/decrement/dispatch chain

sourceAudit.claim: PC-34 keyboard evidence starts with an actual Crawcin value after IO2 normalization, not a route-label transcript.
F0361 can only queue movement when the drained keyboard code matches the PC-34 secondary keyboard table.
A keyboard transcript must prove a matching F0361 queue write and G2153 increment; F0361 entry alone is not enough.
The state delta starts only after F0380 proves non-empty pop/decrement and dispatches to F0365/F0366.
The runtime chain is keyboard-buffer drain, F0361 queueing, F0380 processing, then wait-loop exit.
